title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background is a near-perfect domain match — hybrid AI search, search ranking, personalization, recommendation systems, and experimentation are all core to both her Samsung and Kolon experience, mirroring exactly what Airbnb's R&P team does. Her evidence is strong and specific: precision@3 +30%, CTR +45%, AOV +25%, and 10M+ monthly users demonstrate impact at meaningful scale. The only honest gap is years: the role asks for 10+ years of PM experience with 3+ years on ML/AI products, and Riley has ~9 total years (with ~2 years at the senior AI search level at Samsung), putting her slightly below the stated bar — though her quality of experience is competitive enough to warrant applying.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain overlap in search ranking, personalization, and AI-powered discovery with quantified, scale-level evidence — a slight YOE gap is unlikely to disqualify a candidate this well-matched.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in search relevance, personalization, and recommendation systems that drive measurable marketplace outcomes — including hybrid AI search at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users, CTR +20%) and ML-driven recommendation engines on a $2B+ GMV platform. Proven ability to partner with ML engineers and applied researchers to define model strategy, evaluation frameworks, and experimentation design that directly connect users to the right products at global scale.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing with data scientists, engineers, and UX designers
gap_handling: The role states 10+ years; Riley has ~9. In the cover letter and intro, frame total years as "9+ years" and emphasize that 6+ of those years have been in ML/AI-adjacent product work — the quality and recency of Samsung's AI search work is more relevant than raw tenure. Avoid dwelling on the gap; lead with the domain depth instead.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience is a near-perfect match for the 8+ year requirement, and her core expertise in AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and recommendation systems maps almost exactly to what Airbnb is building on its homepage. Her Samsung work — launching hybrid AI search across international markets, optimizing ranking via behavioral signals, and building recommendation APIs — directly mirrors the role's mandate to apply AI/relevance models to a browsing discovery surface at scale. The modest gap is the consumer marketplace / travel vertical (Airbnb is a two-sided marketplace, not e-commerce) and the role's emphasis on product narrative/marketing communications, which is less evidenced in her resume.
recommendation_reason: Riley's AI search + discovery + personalization stack is a near-ideal fit for Airbnb's homepage transformation, with strong quantitative evidence at scale.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping search, discovery, and personalization experiences that delight users at scale — from hybrid AI search serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung to ML-driven recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV platform. Passionate about applying the latest AI and relevance technologies to surface the right content at the right moment, driving measurable growth in engagement, conversion, and retention.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing.
gap_handling: The role calls for experience crafting external product narratives and working with marketing/media — something less visible in Riley's resume. She should add a brief mention of any GTM storytelling, launch communications, or cross-functional marketing alignment she led (e.g., the conversational AI chat launch or AEO pipeline announcement) to close this perceived gap.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley is a strong match on the core mechanics of this role — consumer e-commerce, A/B experimentation, personalization/recommendation systems, and cross-functional leadership at scale are all deeply evidenced in the resume. The discovery/shopping experience domain aligns tightly with Riley's Samsung and Kolon work. The primary gap is the Health/nutrition/wellness vertical, which is the specific product area this role owns — Riley has no stated experience in health-forward or regulated consumer products, which is a real but bridgeable gap given the preferred (not required) nature of that qualification.
recommendation_reason: Strong match on consumer e-commerce, personalization, experimentation, and zero-to-one shipping; Health domain is a gap but is preferred — not required.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years driving consumer shopping experiences powered by personalization, search, and AI — from zero-to-one launches to scaled products serving millions of users. Proven track record shipping recommendation systems, discovery funnels, and conversion-optimized experiences with measurable impact at Samsung.com, Amazon, and a $2B GMV e-commerce platform.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy — increased PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reduced bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planned and led a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Health/nutrition vertical is the only meaningful gap, but it's listed as preferred — not required. In your cover letter and resume summary, draw a direct line from your work on dietary-adjacent discovery flows (finder experiences, guided shopping, product taxonomy) to the health-forward discovery mission. Emphasize your zero-to-one track record (conversational AI launch, AEO pipeline, virtual try-on) to demonstrate comfort building in ambiguity — a key signal Instacart is hiring for.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, ranking, relevance metrics, and discovery at Samsung.com is a strong match for Faire's core mandate — owning search algorithms for a high-scale marketplace. The domain (B2B wholesale discovery) is adjacent but not identical to Riley's B2C/D2C experience, and the role is explicitly "Staff PM" — one level above Riley's target Senior/PM III tier. The biggest gap is the B2B marketplace context and the "Staff" seniority bar, which at a Series D-stage company like Faire is a meaningful stretch.
recommendation_reason: Excellent search domain alignment and strong evidence bullets, but the Staff PM seniority is a stretch and the B2B wholesale marketplace context is a domain gap worth acknowledging.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in search algorithms, ranking systems, and e-commerce discovery at scale — shipping hybrid AI search for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven catalog tools for 2M+ B2B sellers at Amazon. Proven track record of defining relevance metrics, running high-velocity experimentation, and translating complex ML systems into measurable business outcomes across global marketplaces.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and 7+ years at a "high-growth tech startup" bar are the two real friction points. Riley's 9 years of experience clears the timeline, but Samsung is a large enterprise — emphasize the startup-like ownership and velocity of the Samsung.com digital product team, and lean on the Amazon tenure as the high-growth tech credential. If applying, explicitly frame the Samsung role as a small, agile team within a large org to counter the "startup" signal gap.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product depth, search and discovery expertise, and strong track record with A/B testing and funnel optimization are excellent signals for this role. However, the role is explicitly a Growth PM position focused on developer/API platforms (onboarding, console, billing, self-serve upgrade paths), and Riley's portfolio skews heavily toward consumer-facing search and e-commerce rather than developer-facing platform growth. The Amazon stint shows platform/B2B instincts, but the core ask — owning acquisition/activation/monetization for a developer API — is a meaningful archetype gap that requires specific framing to bridge.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and analytical credentials meet the bar, but Riley needs to aggressively reframe B2B/platform experience to address the developer growth PM archetype gap.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth across AI-powered search, e-commerce, and developer-facing platforms — including ML-driven automation tools for 2M+ Amazon sellers and hybrid AI search serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven expertise in full-funnel optimization, A/B experimentation, and shipping technically complex products at scale, with deep passion for building safe, high-impact AI systems.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — demonstrating self-serve platform product instincts directly analogous to API console and onboarding flows.

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ — evidence of rigorous funnel analytics and KPI ownership.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces via A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion — showing cross-functional growth execution at scale.
gap_handling: The developer/API growth archetype is the key gap. In your cover letter and resume, explicitly map your Amazon experience (self-serve seller tools, onboarding optimization, ML-driven automation for a technical user base) to the developer persona — sellers navigating a complex listing API are a reasonable analog to developers navigating the Claude Platform. Quantify any API-adjacent work and use language like "self-serve upgrade paths," "activation funnel," and "developer experience" to signal fluency with the role's core vocabulary.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background — particularly conversational AI, LLM pipelines, AEO/structured data pipelines, and search ranking — maps well to Twilio's Copilot backend vision, especially the RAG/knowledge layer and contextual AI requirements. However, the role is titled "Principal PM" and explicitly targets backend platform infrastructure (API orchestration, high-concurrency LLM systems, developer tooling), which skews heavier on platform/infra PM archetype than Riley's consumer-facing discovery and e-commerce work. The B2B developer persona and telecom/CPaaS domain are also a meaningful gap, though Twilio's emphasis on AI fluency partially offsets this.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM credentials and conversational AI delivery experience are a genuine fit, but the Principal-level seniority, backend platform/infrastructure focus, and B2B developer-tools persona are real stretches from Riley's current profile.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to automated knowledge pipelines — that drive measurable engagement and deflection at scale. Experienced partnering with ML engineers and data scientists to build context-aware, personalized AI experiences for millions of users, with a track record of translating complex backend logic into high-impact customer journeys.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The biggest gap is the backend platform/infrastructure PM archetype and B2B developer persona — Riley's work has been consumer/merchant-facing, not API-layer or CPaaS. To address this in a cover letter or interview, explicitly connect the AEO pipeline (structured data extraction, schema markup automation) and recommendation API development to "building backend knowledge layers," and frame the conversational AI launch as evidence of owning the full AI agent stack end-to-end, not just the UX.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's core DNA — AI-powered personalization, search/discovery, LLM product experience, and rigorous A/B experimentation — maps strongly onto the AI Personalization mandate. The Samsung and Kolon recommendation system work directly proves consumer-scale personalization. However, this role is titled "Staff PM" and explicitly scoped as a platform/infrastructure product (RAG pipelines, retrieval schemas, contextual signals infrastructure, multi-surface extensibility), which is meaningfully above Riley's target seniority and heavier on platform architecture than her search/discovery-facing work. The customer support domain is also a lateral stretch from e-commerce discovery.
recommendation_reason: Strong personalization and AI credentials, but the Staff-level platform infrastructure scope and CS domain are a stretch — worth applying with a sharply tailored narrative.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping personalization systems, LLM-powered experiences, and search infrastructure at consumer scale — including recommendation APIs, hybrid AI search for 10M+ monthly users, and ML-driven discovery features that moved AOV and retention metrics. Proven cross-functional leader who partners directly with ML engineers and data scientists to define retrieval architectures, experiment frameworks, and multi-surface rollout strategies.
top_bullets: Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform/infrastructure framing — Riley's experience is product-facing rather than platform-layer (retrieval schemas, RAG pipelines, multi-surface extensibility). In the resume and cover letter, explicitly reframe the AEO pipeline (PIM → schema markup → structured data) and the recommendation API work as *platform products* that powered multiple downstream surfaces. Name-drop RAG and contextual retrieval in the context of Samsung's hybrid search and ranking work to signal architectural fluency with ML engineers.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML, personalization, and discovery experience align meaningfully with this role's emphasis on AI-powered self-service and discovery-led product thinking. However, this role is squarely centered on Help/Support experience, knowledge base redesign, and CustOps tooling — a domain Riley has no direct resume signal in. The Growth context and stakeholder management chops are a strong match, but the NYC hybrid requirement creates a location friction point for a Bellevue-based candidate, and sponsorship is unaddressed.
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/discovery/growth overlap but zero explicit Help Center or support-enablement experience creates a meaningful gap in the role's core domain.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving discovery-led, AI-powered product experiences at scale — from conversational AI and hybrid search to personalization systems serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record leading cross-functional teams through complex platform redesigns with measurable impact on user engagement, conversion, and retention across B2C and B2B funnels.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: Riley lacks direct Help Center / knowledge base / support-tooling experience. To bridge this gap, frame the Samsung on-site conversational AI chat launch as a self-service deflection surface, and emphasize the AEO pipeline as structuring and surfacing content for end-users at scale — both map conceptually to what this role does. Also lean into the discovery-led product work and multi-stakeholder alignment at Samsung and Amazon to compensate for the missing CustOps vocabulary.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product experience, proven search and recommendation chops, and solid SQL/data tooling credentials — all of which map reasonably well to Lyft's AI Platform role. However, the role is explicitly Staff-level and centers on internal AI platform/infra product management (agentic systems, multi-agent orchestration, platform evangelism) — a "Platform PM" archetype that is only partially represented in Riley's background, which skews toward consumer-facing search and discovery. The explicit requirement for agentic AI platform ownership, SaaS vendor/contract experience, and CXO-level enterprise presentation cadence are areas where Riley's resume doesn't yet show direct evidence.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML credentials and data tooling overlap, but the Staff-level internal platform PM archetype and agentic AI platform ownership are meaningful gaps requiring deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered systems — from hybrid search and LLM-driven pipelines to recommendation APIs and agentic data workflows — at scale across Samsung and Amazon. Proven track record of translating complex ML capabilities into measurable business outcomes, partnering with scientists and engineers, and reporting progress to senior leadership across global organizations.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers at Amazon, identifying key issues through 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys and partnering with 5 dependent teams to develop scalable solutions

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: Riley's AEO pipeline work (multi-step automation, structured data extraction, LLM-driven pipelines) is the closest proxy for agentic systems thinking — lean into that explicitly. For the platform/internal-customer angle, the Amazon B2B listing tool role (serving 2M+ sellers as internal/external users, partnering with 5 dependent teams) is the best bridge. Acknowledge the Staff-level stretch honestly in cover letter: position this as a deliberate move from consumer AI product into platform AI product, with concrete evidence of cross-functional evangelism and metric reporting to leadership at Samsung.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths — AI search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — are a strong domain signal for Anthropic's AI mission, and the 9 years of experience clears the 6+ year bar comfortably. However, this role is squarely a Growth/Monetization PM role requiring deep ownership of subscription pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion funnels, and consumption-based billing — none of which appear prominently in Riley's resume. The closest transferable signals are growth-adjacent (AOV optimization, first-purchase rate, CRM/retention at Kolon), but Riley has not explicitly owned monetization strategy at scale for a SaaS or subscription product. That gap is the primary risk factor pulling the score down.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and seniority fit, but Riley lacks direct subscription monetization / pricing & packaging ownership that this role explicitly requires — tailoring is essential before applying.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable revenue and engagement growth through data-driven experimentation, personalization, and conversion funnel optimization across e-commerce and AI-powered products. Proven track record of owning end-to-end product strategy at scale — from A/B-tested onboarding flows that lifted first-purchase rates 30% to recommendation systems that grew AOV 25% on a $2B+ GMV platform — now focused on bringing that growth instinct to AI-native monetization at a frontier model company.
top_bullets: Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels — increasing NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The core gap is explicit monetization ownership: pricing strategy, packaging tiers, and free-to-paid conversion for a subscription/API business. In your cover letter and resume summary, draw a direct line from your growth metrics (first-purchase rate +30%, AOV +25%, retention +7%, conversion rate improvements) to monetization outcomes. Frame your Kolon onboarding and CRM work as "conversion and retention funnel ownership." If you have any experience with paywall decisions, plan upsells, or pricing experiments — even informally — surface it explicitly. Consider adding a brief "Growth & Monetization" skills section to your resume.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops, SQL fluency, and hands-on experience with LLM-driven pipelines (AEO, conversational AI, hybrid search) satisfy the AI product requirements well. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS with a heavy emphasis on BI tooling, semantic layers, data warehouses, and the modern data stack — domains Riley has not worked in directly. The 0-to-1 enterprise analytics product angle is also a meaningful departure from Riley's e-commerce/consumer-facing AI search background, and the New York location is outside Riley's preferred Seattle/Remote footprint.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and data pipeline credentials, but the BI/semantic layer/modern data stack domain is a real gap and the NYC-hybrid requirement is a location stretch.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building LLM-driven pipelines, AI search systems, and data-intensive product experiences at Samsung and Amazon. Proven track record owning 0-to-1 AI features — from conversational query interfaces to automated structured-data pipelines — that deliver measurable business impact at scale for millions of users.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines that translated seller inputs into structured catalog templates across 20+ high-impact categories
gap_handling: The clearest gap is BI/analytics domain knowledge — semantic layers, data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, dbt), and governance tooling are central to this role. In your cover letter and interviews, bridge this by emphasizing your AEO metadata pipeline (structured data extraction at scale), your CDP/BigQuery work at Kolon, and your SQL + BigQuery proficiency. title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and AEO systems — that map well to Okta's AI-native product mandate. However, the role requires 10+ years of PM experience and is titled "Principal," placing it above Riley's target seniority (Senior/PM III), and the domain is enterprise identity/security (B2B SaaS), not e-commerce search or consumer AI. There is also no meaningful overlap with Okta's core identity, IAM, IT/HR/Finance workflow automation space, which is central to this role.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM shipping credentials are a real asset, but Principal-level title, enterprise identity domain gap, and 9 vs. 10+ years required experience make this a competitive stretch requiring significant resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-native experiences — from LLM-powered pipelines and conversational AI to hybrid search and agentic discovery systems — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Proven track record taking AI products from 0→1 innovation through broad enterprise adoption, with rigorous evaluation frameworks and measurable business outcomes.

score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops and experience shipping LLM-powered systems (conversational AI, hybrid search, AEO pipelines) are directly relevant to Anthropic's frontier AI product space, and the domain alignment with Claude Code's AI-native positioning is strong. However, the role has a hard requirement of at least 1 year as a professional engineer, and there is no engineering background in Riley's resume — this is the single biggest gap and a likely screener. Additionally, Claude Code is a developer tools product targeting software engineers, an archetype (dev tools / CLI / SDK) that Riley has no direct experience in; Riley's PM work has been in e-commerce, search, and B2B seller tools rather than developer-facing products.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product background and sponsorship alignment, but the engineering background requirement and zero dev tools experience are significant gaps that make this a long-shot worth applying to only with a highly tailored narrative.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from hybrid search and conversational AI to automated structured-data pipelines — at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

If you have even hobbyist or coursework coding experience (Purdue MSBAIM likely included Python/stats), name it explicitly. title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant. However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting AI-driven product discovery for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system using multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is IAM domain knowledge (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust). Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

score_rationale: This role is a Senior Group Product Manager — a people-management role requiring a team of PMs and deep ads marketplace/ad tech expertise (bidding, targeting, pacing, budget optimization). Riley has zero people management experience on the resume, which is a hard blocker for a GPM role. While Riley has strong AI, search, and e-commerce experimentation chops, there is no direct ad tech experience (auction mechanics, advertiser-facing tools, bidding/pacing systems), which is the core domain requirement. The seniority level (GPM = Director-adjacent) is explicitly outside Riley's target and avoid range.
